checkpoint.contextAtWrite: This checkpoint closes out a multi-day, multi-feature session (2026-07-15 through 2026-07-16) spanning 5 merged PRs across the 2026-07-09-beta-readiness-infra epic and 4 standalone short-track stories. Session summary: (1) bri-s3.3 (PR #483, merged) -- second DoR re-run found tir-s9 had shipped the fix for the login-identityKey defect found on 2026-07-13; re-scoped to GitHub-org-allowlist mode and unblocked; implementation dispatched, a real acVerified type bug and a suite-order test flake were found and fixed post-merge. (2) tst-s1 (PR #484, merged) -- triaged all 69 pre-existing baseline test failures unmasked by pcr-s1's test runner; 2 fixed, 67 documented by root cause; a local-vs-CI baseline discrepancy found and corrected mid-PR. (3) jlc-s1 (PR #485, merged) -- investigated the operator's live 'Journey limit reached' report; initial diagnosis (based on a stale main-checkout file) was wrong and corrected mid-implementation by the dispatched agent itself; real fix persists bri-s3.5's paid-plan bypass to Postgres so it survives a server restart; verified end-to-end against a real running server. (4) cfg-s1 (PR #486, merged) -- follow-up from tst-s1; confirmed and fixed the root cause of 3 of 5 local-vs-CI test divergences (an unscoped git-grep silently no-oping on Windows, executing-and-false-positiving on real CI); 2 files remain unresolved (Node version mismatch suspected). An outer-loop completeness review then found: workspace/state.json stale since 2026-07-05 (this write closes that gap); no short-track story had ever reached /definition-of-done (resolved: DoD artefacts retroactively written for pcr-s1, stis-s1, tst-s1, jlc-s1, cfg-s1; CLAUDE.md's short-track routing line updated to state Step 8 still applies); 18 capture-log entries (12 pre-session, 6 from this session) promoted to workspace/learnings.md.
checkpoint.resumeInstruction: No feature is currently mid-pipeline. All 5 stories from this session (pcr-s1, stis-s1, tst-s1, jlc-s1, cfg-s1) are merged and at definition-of-done. Two known open items, neither blocking: (1) tests/run-gpa-tests.js and tests/check-gpa-sc06-source-path-guard.js remain unresolved local-vs-CI test divergences -- leading hypothesis is a Node 20 (CI) vs Node 22 (local) mismatch, not yet confirmed. (2) definition-of-ready/SKILL.md's H-GOV check still lacks an explicit short-track exception clause (recurred as a workaround across 5 stories this session) -- a good candidate for its own short-track fix. Start the next feature's /discovery or address either open item.
